assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan and supporting conventions.md reference patterns from at least 3 of the listed files: (1) advisory/endpoints/get.rs — plan.md line 42: 'Extracts path params via Path<Id> (matching pattern from get.rs)' and file-4-description.md: 'Follows the exact pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs'; (2) advisory/service/advisory.rs — plan.md line 22: 'matching existing fetch/list signature pattern'; (3) advisory/model/summary.rs — plan.md line 61: 'Entity AdvisorySummary.severity -- Implementation Notes references model/summary.rs'; (4) common/src/error.rs — conventions.md line 17: 'AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs'. The conventions.md header 'Discovered Conventions (from sibling analysis)' explicitly indicates existing code was analyzed.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly 6 files: 3 to modify (advisory/model/mod.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs) and 3 to create (advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs). The plan explicitly states in 'Files NOT Modified' section: 'server/src/main.rs -- no changes needed'. No unrelated files appear anywhere in the plan.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 35-51 list: File 4 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE), File 5 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE), File 6 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (CREATE). Each has a corresponding file-N-description.md with full implementation details.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 17-31 list: File 1 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' (MODIFY — add pub mod severity_summary), File 2 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (MODIFY — add severity_summary method), File 3 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (MODIFY — register new route). Each has a corresponding file-N-description.md with detailed change descriptions.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 69-78 show commit message: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' (Conventional Commits format with type=feat, scope=api) followed by a body and footer 'Implements TC-9201' referencing the Jira ticket.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: The plan has a '## Target Branch' section (line 8-9) identifying 'main' as the target branch, but there is no mention anywhere in the plan or supporting files of creating a branch named TC-9201, nor any reference to a branch naming convention. The plan skips the branch creation step entirely.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md line 6-8: 'Each domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ directory structure.' conventions.md lines 16-18: '### Error Handling - All handlers return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs. Errors are wrapped with .context() for descriptive error messages propagated to the caller.' Both patterns are explicitly identified with specific file references.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 8-9 contain a dedicated section: '## Target Branch\n\nmain'. This explicitly identifies 'main' as the target branch.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: No mention of 'description digest', 'digest comment', 'Step 1.5', 'backward compatibility', or 'description-digest-protocol' appears anywhere in plan.md, conventions.md, or any of the file-N-description.md files. The concept is completely absent from all outputs.
